bulletins.highlights: Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches easily.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and warming a dangerous avalanche situation will persist.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northwesterly wind, further wind slabs formed in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies on west, north and east facing slopes. The avalanche prone locations are numerous, especially in areas close to the tree line, as well as above the tree line. Winter sport participants can release avalanches easily, including large ones. Natural avalanches are still even now possible.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation loose snow avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes. In particular on steep, rather lightly snow-covered east and west facing slopes the wet avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach large size in isolated cases.

In addition gliding avalanches are possible. This applies especially on cut and grassy slopes below approximately 2000 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Tuesday. The wind was strong to storm force in some cases. The wind has transported the new snow significantly. Further wind slabs formed. On Wednesday it will be very mild.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes in areas close to the tree line, and above the tree line.

The rain gave rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Hardly any decrease in danger of dry avalanches. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding avalanches require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.
The older wind slabs can be released in isolated cases at intermediate and high altitudes. These can in some places be released by people and reach medium size.
On steep grassy slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible. Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.
Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.
Since Friday the weather has been very mild. In particular below the tree line: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be very mild. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Moist avalanches can in some cases release the wet snowpack and reach large size in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Rapid increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The weather will be mild. In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: At low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2800 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers.

The older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.
Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.

In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

Since Friday the weather has been very mild. In particular below the tree line: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be very mild. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Ground avalanches are possible in particular in the afternoon.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Significant increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The weather will be very warm. On very steep sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

The somewhat older wind slabs can be released in some cases. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and can mostly be released by large loads, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.
Isolated medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last three days.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be very warm. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Ground avalanches are possible in particular in the afternoon.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be warm. The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
On sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. In particular below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

Gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible on steep grassy slopes.

The weather conditions fostered a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and can be released in some cases by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.
During the day: The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be very warm. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Moist avalanches can in some cases release the wet snowpack and reach large size in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The weather will be very mild. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural moist avalanches are possible, in particular in steep rocky terrain, as well as on cut and grassy slopes. Snow slides on road cut slopes.
Careful route selection is recommended. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.

In addition the no longer entirely fresh wind slabs must be taken into account. They are in some cases prone to triggering. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In particular here the avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The weather conditions fostered a settling of the near-surface layers. In its middle, the snowpack is unfavourably layered.

The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 3300 m.

Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. There are large frames on the crest.
In these regions at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year. Small and medium-sized moist avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of natural wet avalanches will not decrease for the time being.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will still be encountered.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on sunny slopes.
Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment: The weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes. Wind slabs remain in some cases prone to triggering. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The weather will be very mild. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning.

With the freezing level at 3300 metres, combined with solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days. Careful route selection is recommended. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.

As a consequence of the strong wind, snow drift accumulations formed. They can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. The avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 3300 m.

The weather conditions fostered a settling of the near-surface layers. In its middle, the snowpack is unfavourably layered. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Small and medium-sized moist avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will not decrease for the time being.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. In particular, however, the danger of wet avalanches will increase.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above approximately 1800 m. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

The fresh wind slabs are prone to triggering in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are to be expected. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes, and at the base of rock walls. The runout zones are to be treated with caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good at times. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies especially on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2400 m.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the activity of wet avalanches will appreciably increase.

bulletins.highlights: Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution. Very steep sunny slopes: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls large surface-area wind slabs formed. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
On steep shady slopes the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular above approximately 1900 m clearly visible wind slabs formed.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep north, east and west facing slopes.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2400 m.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase.

bulletins.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution. Very steep sunny slopes: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. In particular at high altitude avalanche prone wind slabs formed, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.
On very steep sunny slopes medium-sized natural avalanches must be expected. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Mostly the dry avalanches are medium-sized and can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs of last week are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.
The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering over a wide area. The avalanche danger will persist. The activity of wet avalanches will gradually increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1800 m. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released easily. Wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2000 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.
Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported some snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are the main danger. Slab avalanches are possible in isolated cases as before.
avalancheActivity.comment: Caution is to be exercised in areas with glide cracks. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of gliding avalanches being released will increase quickly in particular on steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes. Gliding avalanches are possible at any time, even large ones in isolated cases. They can also be released in the morning. In addition on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes, further medium-sized slab avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation individual small and medium-sized wet avalanches are to be expected.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good over a wide area. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The wind slabs can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming moist small and medium sized avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
And in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2000 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on steep south facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually on steep south facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Single backcountry tourers can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack will consolidate.
